page: Microsoft Azure FX24ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the FXmsv2 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at a high base frequency of 4.2 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by a massive 260 MB L3 cache. This server is equipped with 504.0 GB of RAM, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

Performance benchmarks reveal strong capabilities in single-threaded operations and specific computational tasks. The processor's advanced instruction support, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions, enables strong performance in text processing, single-threaded gzip compression, and image processing. In LLM inference tests using a small 135M model, the server achieved top-tier text generation speeds of 420.68 tokens per second and strong prompt processing speeds. However, multi-core benchmarks, such as stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core, align with average tier performance, and Passmark memory scores indicate a relative weakness in certain memory-intensive synthetic tests despite the large capacity.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX24ms_v2 offers a specialized hardware profile that trades local storage for massive memory density and high-frequency dedicated compute. This makes it highly suitable for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe drives but benefit from premium remote storage. Recommended workloads include large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise application servers, text processing pipelines, and light language model inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure FX24ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine from the FXmsv2 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 24 dedicated vCPUs and 12 physical cores. It features an expansive 504.0 GB of RAM, providing 21.0 GB per vCPU, alongside a 260 MB L3 cache. While the instance lacks local storage, it is premium storage capable and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in text processing and small-model LLM text generation, alongside strong single-threaded compression and image processing. Conversely, multi-core computational performance and synthetic memory benchmarks remain in the average to weak ranges. This hardware profile is highly optimized for memory-heavy workloads, making it an excellent fit for large in-memory databases, enterprise applications, and text-processing pipelines that leverage remote premium storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX24ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs, a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, and 504 GB of RAM, optimized for large-scale in-memory workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX24ms_v2 is a memory-intensive VM with 24 dedicated vCPUs, 504 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, built for in-memory workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-intensive virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and massive memory capacity for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor
504.0 GB of RAM providing a high 21.0 GB per vCPU memory density
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations
Top-tier performance in text processing and small-model LLM text generation
Premium storage capable configuration without bundled local storage
Best suited for in-memory databases, enterprise applications, and text pipelines
